other_observers: Oliver Komar, Cole Wild, Tony Leukering, Lary Semo, Paul Lehman, Steve and Joe Mammoser, Joe Himmel, Dick Maxfield, Rachel Hopper; Tony Leukering
description: A gull about the size of average California Gull (estimated 21 inches in length), based oon close proximity of this bird to several California gulls of various sizes, numerous Ring-billed Gulls, and singletons of immature Herring Gull and Lesser Black-backed Gull. Head all white with dark eye and smallish mostly black bill with flesh-colored base. Mantle whitish with faint pale brown mottling. Underparts whitish with faint brown barring on vent. Wings whitish with faint brown mottling on coverts, and secondaries. Tertials and primaries appeared (at a great distance) to be pale gray centered with broad white edges, without obvious barring or mottling. Pink legs. When sitting, primaries extended moderately far beyond the tail (more than for Herring Gull, less than for Lesser Black-backed Gull).Behaviors: standing, floatingCall: nonePlumage: first cycle immature
